title: Symplocos phaeoclados
intro: Symplocos phaeoclados, also known as the Indian alstonia or Symplocos tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its dark green, glossy leaves and showy white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Symplocos phaeoclados include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory conditions.
